scope.purpose: Make railway-language confidence visible and prevent an uncertain or frozen legacy term from silently becoming a successor UI, schema or API convention.
scope.bounded_claim: This contract records accepted project usage, provisional descriptions, review-required semantic families and frozen legacy identifiers. It does not claim that lexical scanning can prove railway correctness or that an accepted term is universally correct outside its declared context.
scope.product_effect: Control evidence only. B14 and B15 remain byte-identical, no successor source or UI is created and no unresolved railway term is renamed.
scope.human_factor_rule: When a contributor is unsure, preserve the observed wording, record its context and mark it for review instead of guessing or performing a mechanical replacement.

state_definitions.meaning: Approved for the bounded project context and preferred forms recorded here; this is not a claim about every railway, era or jurisdiction.
state_definitions.new_use: Allowed only in the declared context. A changed meaning or broader public claim reopens review.

state_definitions.meaning: A visible working description whose precise prototype or railway meaning is not yet evidenced.
state_definitions.new_use: May be used only with its provisional status visible; it cannot become a production identifier or public factual claim.

state_definitions.meaning: The wording is ambiguous, source-specific or plausibly wrong in the proposed context.
state_definitions.new_use: Blocked in successor public UI, schema and API surfaces until the named railway-domain review closes.

state_definitions.meaning: An exact existing token is retained solely for immutable source, evidence reproducibility or compatibility.
state_definitions.new_use: Blocked outside the enumerated legacy boundary; a compatibility translation must expose the canonical successor term.

policy.new_term_rule: A new railway term or a materially new context must be added as accepted, provisional or review-required before it enters a successor public UI, persisted schema, production manifest or public API.
policy.marker_rule: Development code or prose may use a TERM-REVIEW marker only for a registered provisional or review-required term and must add a matching open-review record with an owner and closure gate.
policy.promotion_rule: Only the project owner may accept a terminology decision, using recorded railway/source evidence and the exact bounded context. Tests or implementation convenience cannot promote a term.
policy.legacy_rule: Do not rename immutable macros or frozen evidence identifiers. New modular surfaces use canonical wording and translate legacy identifiers only in an explicit compatibility adapter.
policy.review_output_rule: A review records accepted wording, rejected alternatives, semantic context, evidence, reviewer, decision date and affected locations; unresolved findings remain visible.
policy.automation_limit: The validator detects registered-state drift, known prohibited tokens, unregistered frozen paths and missing review ownership. Human railway-domain review decides contextual correctness.

terms.display: plain line / plain-line / plain line track
terms.bounded_meaning: Track without switches and crossings; plain describes the infrastructure class, not whether the alignment is straight.
terms.context_rule: Use plain line as a noun and plain-line as a compound modifier. Curved, transitioned and multiple-track layouts may still be plain line.
terms.later_gate: Reopen only if the bounded meaning or cited railway authority changes.

terms.bounded_meaning: The infrastructure class contrasted with plain line in this project; turnouts and crossovers are within the project's S&C or special-trackwork scope.
terms.context_rule: Do not use the class label to erase the more specific turnout, crossover, switch or crossing component meaning.
terms.later_gate: Phase 8 component-language review retains the class/component distinction.

terms.bounded_meaning: Alignment descriptors independent of the plain-line versus S&C classification.
terms.context_rule: Never use straight as a synonym for plain line.

terms.bounded_meaning: Project alignment descriptors for gradual geometric change, with the exact mathematical subtype stated where behaviour depends on it.
terms.context_rule: Do not imply that every transition is an Euler easement; name the implemented curve family when it matters.
terms.later_gate: Phase 7 alignment terminology and behaviour review.

terms.bounded_meaning: A longitudinal position on a declared centreline or route within the current geometry model.
terms.context_rule: State the owning centreline, origin and direction. Do not use station without context where it could mean a passenger station or structure.

terms.bounded_meaning: A layout containing more than one track alignment; it does not determine whether any alignment is plain line or S&C.
terms.context_rule: Keep track count separate from alignment shape and S&C classification.
terms.later_gate: Phase 7 multiple-track UI and schema review.

terms.bounded_meaning: A historical project identifier for plain-line evidence and compatibility paths, not the canonical railway category.
terms.context_rule: Retain only in the enumerated legacy paths, immutable source quotations and explicit compatibility references.
terms.later_gate: Retire only after every evidence link and compatibility caller has an accepted migration.

terms.bounded_meaning: Unresolved legacy phrase that may mean plain-line, independent single-road, non-shared or another support class.
terms.context_rule: Do not choose a replacement until timber semantics and source usage are reviewed.
terms.later_gate: Phase 8 timber classification and public-label review.

terms.bounded_meaning: A legacy REA/Templot-derived chair-family expression whose exact prototype and source meaning is not established by the plain-line decision.
terms.context_rule: Do not mechanically replace it with plain-line chair or treat it as a cleared prototype classification.
terms.owner_role: Phase 9 chair evidence and railway-domain reviewer
terms.later_gate: S1-07, S1-08 and S1-10 evidence decisions in Phase 9.

terms.bounded_meaning: Support terms whose correct use may depend on plain line, S&C, prototype practice, component length and the source convention.
terms.context_rule: Do not treat sleeper and timber as universal synonyms; review public labels and schema meanings against the represented prototype.
terms.later_gate: Phase 8 support taxonomy, UI and migration review.

terms.bounded_meaning: Related but context-sensitive S&C terms that must not be assumed to be interchangeable; points is also heavily used for generic geometry vertices in current code.
terms.context_rule: A public railway label must identify whether it means a complete turnout, a switch assembly, movable rails or generic geometric points.
terms.later_gate: Phase 8 S&C component taxonomy and UI review.

terms.bounded_meaning: Crossing-component terminology varies by source and audience; frog is not an established project term and is prohibited from silently becoming the default label.
terms.context_rule: Use the current specific component term only when its geometry is known; any proposed frog label requires explicit railway-domain review.
evidence: Current B14/B15 common-crossing and vee terminology
terms.later_gate: Phase 8 S&C component taxonomy and UI review.

terms.bounded_meaning: Working project description for the first assisted chair pilot; no precise company, standard, LMS or REA attribution is asserted.
terms.context_rule: Keep the provisional status visible and do not publish it as a precise prototype package designation.

macro_findings.disposition: Replace with plain-line wording only in an approved successor UI while preserving B14/B15 evidence.
Determine the represented support class before selecting successor wording.
Resolve through the chair evidence and constituent review; do not inherit the phrase as canonical data.

open_reviews.question: Does the phrase mean plain-line, independent single-road, non-shared or another support classification?
open_reviews.closure_gate: Recorded source evidence, accepted replacement or retained bounded term, and successor UI/schema review.

open_reviews.question: What exact source classification does ordinary carry for the S1 family, and what independently evidenced successor term is appropriate?
open_reviews.owner_role: Phase 9 chair evidence and railway-domain reviewer
open_reviews.closure_gate: S1-07, S1-08 and S1-10 close with field/component provenance and accepted package terminology.

open_reviews.question: Which support categories and public labels apply across plain line, turnout and crossover workflows?
open_reviews.closure_gate: Phase 8 support taxonomy maps every successor label and persisted field to an evidenced meaning.

open_reviews.question: Which current public labels refer to a complete turnout, a switch assembly, movable rails or generic geometry points?
open_reviews.closure_gate: Phase 8 component taxonomy and UI review records each public meaning without renaming generic point variables mechanically.

open_reviews.question: Which crossing-component terms are correct for the represented UK prototype and intended audience?
open_reviews.closure_gate: Phase 8 accepts the component glossary and rejects or bounds alternatives before public use.

open_reviews.question: What exact prototype/company/standard designation is supported by the selected primary evidence?
open_reviews.closure_gate: S1-07 closes with rights-compatible primary evidence and explicit owner confirmation.

evidenced: The four assurance states and their promotion rules are explicit.
Known ordinary-track, timber and chair findings are bounded without changing B14/B15.
Every unresolved semantic family has an accountable owner and later gate.
Successor product surfaces fail on known prohibited legacy tokens unless a reviewed exception is registered.
The human review workflow states what automation cannot decide.
not_claimed: No open terminology review is resolved by this contract.
No term is accepted outside its bounded context.
Phase 1 acceptance does not resolve or waive any of the six named later reviews.
